title: Atalantia citrifolia
intro: Atalantia citrifolia, also known as the citron tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to Southeast Asia and widely cultivated for its edible fruit. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antidiabetic properties, making it a promising natural remedy for various ailments. The bioactive constituents of Atalantia citrifolia include flavonoids, limonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat digestive issues, fever, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Atalantia citrifolia is classified in the family Rutaceae, commonly known as the citrus family. This classification places it among the flowering plants and more specifically, within the order Sapindales. The family Rutaceae consists of approximately 160 genera and 1,000 species of plants, many of which are well-known for their fragrant flowers and edible fruits. Atalantia citrifolia is a species of flowering plant native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. Further classification of the species includes its placement in the genus Atalantia, which contains approximately 10 species of plants.
uses: Atalantia citrifolia has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Its leaves and fruits are known to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its medicinal value. In some Asian cultures, the plant is used to treat digestive issues and reduce swelling. The plant's extracts have also been studied for their potential antioxidant and antiproliferative effects. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal properties and uses of Atalantia citrifolia.

constituents: Atalantia citrifolia contains flavonoids, limonoids, and other terpenoids as its active constituents. These compounds are responsible for the plant's medicinal properties. Flavonoids, in particular, have been found to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. Limonoids, on the other hand, have been reported to have hepatoprotective and anticancer effects.
